words.simpleMeaning: to have or own something

sentences.daily: I possess a car.
sentences.office: She possesses great analytical skills.
sentences.interview: I possess strong problem-solving abilities.
sentences.business: The company possesses advanced technology.
sentences.formal: He possesses vast knowledge of law.
sentences.informal: I possess just a bike, nothing fancy.

words.simpleMeaning: to have something that belongs to you

sentences.daily: I own a small house.
sentences.office: She owns her own business.
sentences.interview: I own my professional development.
sentences.business: They own multiple properties.
sentences.formal: He owns a large estate.
sentences.informal: I own a scooter, that's it.

words.simpleMeaning: able to be used or obtained

sentences.daily: I have time available today.
sentences.office: Are you available for a meeting?
sentences.interview: I am available to join immediately.
sentences.business: The product is available online.
sentences.formal: He is available for consultation.

words.simpleMeaning: to be present at an event

sentences.daily: I have a meeting to attend.
sentences.office: She has attended the training session.
sentences.interview: I have attended several industry workshops.
sentences.business: We have to attend the conference in Delhi.
sentences.formal: He attended the international ceremony.

words.simpleMeaning: to finish something entirely

sentences.daily: I have completed my morning routine.
sentences.office: She has completed the quarterly report.
sentences.interview: I have completed my degree from Delhi University.
sentences.business: We have completed the product launch.
sentences.formal: He has completed his doctoral research.

words.simpleMeaning: to reach a place

sentences.daily: He has arrived home late.
sentences.office: Has she arrived at the office yet?
sentences.interview: I arrived five minutes early for the interview.
sentences.business: The shipment has arrived safely.
sentences.formal: He arrived at the venue on time.

words.simpleMeaning: to reach the end of a task

sentences.daily: I haven't finished eating yet.
sentences.office: Has he finished the assignment?
sentences.interview: I finished my MBA last year.
sentences.business: We haven't finished the deal discussion.
sentences.formal: He has finished his research paper.

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully reach a goal

sentences.daily: I have achieved my fitness goal.
sentences.office: She has achieved her sales target.
sentences.interview: I have achieved a 30% cost reduction in my previous role.
sentences.business: We have achieved market leadership.
sentences.formal: He has achieved international recognition.
sentences.informal: I've achieved my dream, at last!

words.simpleMeaning: to gain or obtain something

sentences.daily: I have acquired a new skill.
sentences.office: She has acquired a new client.
sentences.interview: I have acquired expertise in data analysis.
sentences.business: The company has acquired a startup.
sentences.formal: He has acquired extensive knowledge.
sentences.informal: I've acquired a good habit lately.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep something in good condition

sentences.daily: I have maintained a healthy diet.
sentences.office: She maintains a detailed work log.
sentences.interview: I have maintained a consistent performance record.
sentences.business: We maintain strong client relationships.
sentences.formal: He has maintained his integrity throughout.

words.usageNote: 'maintain + noun' very common in professional writing।

words.simpleMeaning: to grow or make something grow

sentences.daily: I have developed a new hobby.
sentences.office: She has developed a training program.
sentences.interview: I have developed strong leadership skills.
sentences.business: We have developed a new product line.
sentences.formal: He has developed a comprehensive strategy.
sentences.informal: I've developed a love for reading.

words.simpleMeaning: to be in charge of or handle something

sentences.daily: I have managed my time well today.
sentences.office: She manages a team of fifteen people.
sentences.interview: I have managed cross-functional teams.
sentences.business: We have managed multiple projects simultaneously.
sentences.formal: He manages the department efficiently.
sentences.informal: I manage to finish everything on time.

words.usageNote: 'manage a team/project' interview में frequently used।

words.simpleMeaning: to show or prove something clearly

sentences.daily: I have demonstrated patience.
sentences.office: She has demonstrated her leadership skills.
sentences.interview: I have demonstrated the ability to work under pressure.
sentences.business: We have demonstrated consistent growth.
sentences.formal: He demonstrated an outstanding performance.

words.simpleMeaning: to hand over or present something officially

sentences.daily: I have submitted my application.
sentences.office: She has submitted the weekly report.
sentences.interview: I have submitted all required documents.
sentences.business: We have submitted our proposal.
sentences.formal: He has submitted the final version.

words.simpleMeaning: to get something that is given or sent

sentences.daily: I have received a gift.
sentences.office: She has received the project brief.
sentences.interview: I have received an offer letter.
sentences.business: We have received payment from the client.
sentences.formal: He received an award for excellence.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something definite or certain

sentences.daily: I have confirmed my appointment.
sentences.office: She has confirmed the meeting details.
sentences.interview: I have confirmed my availability.
sentences.business: We have confirmed the order.
sentences.formal: He has confirmed his attendance.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something better

sentences.daily: I have improved my cooking skills.
sentences.office: She has improved her communication.
sentences.interview: I have improved my technical skills over time.
sentences.business: We have improved our product quality.
sentences.formal: He has improved the system significantly.

words.simpleMeaning: to find a solution to a problem

sentences.office: She has resolved the client complaint.
sentences.interview: I have resolved conflicts within teams.
sentences.business: We have resolved the billing dispute.

sentences.business: We have delivered the project on time.

sentences.informal: I've delivered everything they asked for.

sentences.daily: I have implemented a new routine.
sentences.office: She has implemented the new process.
sentences.interview: I have implemented cost-saving measures.
sentences.business: We have implemented the new software.
sentences.formal: He has implemented the government directive.

sentences.daily: I have launched a new blog.
sentences.office: She has launched the marketing campaign.
sentences.interview: I have launched two mobile apps.
sentences.business: We have launched our new product.

sentences.office: She has proposed a budget increase.
sentences.interview: I have proposed innovative solutions.

sentences.formal: He has proposed an amendment to the policy.

sentences.business: We have negotiated a merger deal.

sentences.business: We have updated our pricing.

sentences.business: We have increased our market share.

sentences.daily: I have expanded my vocabulary.

sentences.interview: I have streamlined operations reducing waste by 30%.

sentences.daily: I have a core competency in communication.
sentences.office: She has demonstrated key competencies.
sentences.interview: I have strong competencies in project management.
sentences.business: We have identified core competencies.
sentences.formal: He has developed new competencies.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to take action independently, or a new plan

sentences.daily: I have taken the initiative.
sentences.office: She has led a new initiative.
sentences.interview: I have initiative and take ownership.
sentences.business: We have launched a CSR initiative.
sentences.formal: He has spearheaded the initiative.

words.simpleMeaning: responsibility for one's actions

sentences.daily: I have accountability for my actions.
sentences.office: She has shown accountability at work.
sentences.interview: I have a strong sense of accountability.
sentences.business: We have built a culture of accountability.
sentences.formal: He has accountability for all outcomes.

words.simpleMeaning: being open and honest, especially in business

sentences.daily: I believe in transparency.
sentences.office: She has maintained transparency with the team.
sentences.interview: I have maintained transparency in all dealings.
sentences.business: We have a transparency policy.
sentences.formal: He has ensured full transparency.

words.simpleMeaning: a high degree of skill or expertise

sentences.daily: I have proficiency in English.
sentences.office: She has shown proficiency in Excel.
sentences.interview: I have proficiency in three programming languages.
sentences.business: We have demonstrated proficiency in our field.
sentences.formal: He has certified proficiency.

words.simpleMeaning: expert skill or knowledge in a field

sentences.daily: I have expertise in cooking.
sentences.office: She has expertise in financial modeling.
sentences.interview: I have expertise in cloud architecture.
sentences.business: We have deep expertise in our domain.
sentences.formal: He has published expertise.
sentences.informal: I've got expertise in this area.

words.simpleMeaning: an official record that someone has finished a course

sentences.daily: I have the qualification for this role.
sentences.office: She has all the required qualifications.
sentences.interview: I have qualifications in both finance and tech.
sentences.business: We have qualified professionals.
sentences.formal: He has academic qualifications.
sentences.informal: I've got the qualification, no worries.

words.simpleMeaning: a person who applies for a job or an election

sentences.daily: I am a candidate for the position.
sentences.office: She has shortlisted five candidates.
sentences.interview: As a candidate, I bring unique strengths.
sentences.business: We have interviewed ten candidates.
sentences.formal: He was the strongest candidate.

words.simpleMeaning: a goal or target that you are working towards

sentences.daily: I have a clear objective for today.
sentences.office: She has outlined the team objectives.
sentences.interview: I have clear short and long-term objectives.
sentences.business: We have achieved our objectives.
sentences.formal: He has set strategic objectives.

words.simpleMeaning: how well someone or something does a task

sentences.daily: I have tracked my performance.
sentences.office: She has reviewed team performance.
sentences.interview: I have consistently delivered strong performance.
sentences.business: We have strong financial performance.
sentences.formal: He has demonstrated exemplary performance.
sentences.informal: My performance has been great lately.

words.simpleMeaning: a duty or obligation to do something

sentences.daily: I have a responsibility to be punctual.
sentences.office: She has taken responsibility for the task.
sentences.interview: I have handled multiple responsibilities.
sentences.business: We have a responsibility to our customers.
sentences.formal: He has fulfilled his responsibility.

words.simpleMeaning: something accomplished with effort and skill

sentences.daily: I have a great achievement to share.
sentences.office: She has listed her achievements.
sentences.interview: I have several achievements I am proud of.
sentences.business: We have many achievements this year.
sentences.formal: He has an excellent record of achievements.
sentences.informal: My biggest achievement was getting this job.

words.simpleMeaning: feeling sure about your abilities

sentences.daily: I have become more confident.
sentences.office: She has a confident presentation style.
sentences.interview: I am confident in my abilities.
sentences.business: We are confident of our product.
sentences.formal: He has a confident demeanor.

words.simpleMeaning: taking action before problems arise

sentences.daily: I have a proactive attitude.
sentences.office: She has been proactive in solving problems.
sentences.interview: I have a proactive approach to work.
sentences.business: We have a proactive customer service.
sentences.formal: He has demonstrated a proactive stance.

words.simpleMeaning: able to change or adapt easily

sentences.daily: I have a flexible schedule.
sentences.office: She has a flexible working style.
sentences.interview: I am flexible and can adapt quickly.
sentences.business: We have flexible pricing options.
sentences.formal: He has a flexible approach.

words.simpleMeaning: able to recover quickly from difficulties

sentences.daily: I have become resilient over time.
sentences.office: She has demonstrated resilience under pressure.
sentences.interview: I have a resilient mindset.
sentences.business: We have a resilient business model.
sentences.formal: He has shown remarkable resilience.

words.simpleMeaning: to do several tasks at the same time

sentences.daily: I have multitasked all morning.
sentences.office: She has multitasked efficiently.
sentences.interview: I have the ability to multitask.
sentences.business: We have multitasked across departments.
sentences.formal: He has multitasked on critical assignments.
sentences.informal: I can multitask like a pro.

words.simpleMeaning: to make a formal request or put something into use

sentences.daily: I have applied for a new job.
sentences.office: She has applied the new policy.
sentences.interview: I have applied my skills in various projects.
sentences.business: We have applied for a business loan.
sentences.formal: He has applied for government clearance.
sentences.informal: I've applied, waiting to hear back.

words.simpleMeaning: to be exceptionally good at something

sentences.daily: I have excelled in my studies.
sentences.office: She has excelled in her role.
sentences.interview: I have excelled in client-facing roles.
sentences.business: We have excelled in customer service.
sentences.formal: He has excelled academically.
sentences.informal: I've excelled at everything I tried.

words.simpleMeaning: to change to suit a new situation

sentences.daily: I have adapted to city life.
sentences.office: She has adapted to remote working.
sentences.interview: I have adapted quickly to new environments.
sentences.business: We have adapted our model post-pandemic.
sentences.formal: He has adapted the curriculum.

words.simpleMeaning: knowledge or skill gained over time

sentences.daily: I have great experience in cooking.
sentences.office: She has experience in project management.
sentences.interview: I have five years of experience in finance.
sentences.business: We have experienced professionals.
sentences.formal: He has extensive experience.

words.usageNote: 'have experience in/of + field' key interview word।

words.simpleMeaning: a quality or ability that makes someone good at something

sentences.daily: I have great physical strength.
sentences.office: She has the strength to manage the team.
sentences.interview: My strength is problem-solving.
sentences.business: We have the strength to compete globally.
sentences.formal: He has demonstrated considerable strength.
sentences.informal: My strength is that I never give up.

words.usageNote: 'my strength is/I have the strength to' interview।

words.simpleMeaning: a quality or ability that is lacking

sentences.daily: I have a weakness for sweets.
sentences.office: She has acknowledged her weakness.
sentences.interview: My weakness is that I sometimes overcommit.
sentences.business: We have identified our weakness.
sentences.formal: He has disclosed his areas of weakness.

words.simpleMeaning: a chance to do something

sentences.daily: I have a great opportunity today.
sentences.office: She has identified a business opportunity.
sentences.interview: I have an opportunity to grow here.
sentences.business: We have a market opportunity.
sentences.formal: He has taken every opportunity.

words.simpleMeaning: the amount of work a person has to do

sentences.daily: I have a heavy workload today.
sentences.office: She has managed a high workload.
sentences.interview: I have managed a high workload with efficiency.
sentences.business: We have increased workload this quarter.
sentences.formal: He has maintained quality despite heavy workload.
sentences.informal: My workload is crazy this week.

words.simpleMeaning: a collection of work samples or investments

sentences.daily: I have an updated portfolio.
sentences.office: She has a strong design portfolio.
sentences.interview: I have a portfolio of projects.
sentences.business: We have a diverse investment portfolio.
sentences.formal: He has submitted his professional portfolio.

words.simpleMeaning: a document listing a person's work experience and qualifications

sentences.daily: I have updated my resume.
sentences.office: She has a well-written resume.
sentences.interview: I have a strong resume.
sentences.business: We have resumes of all applicants.
sentences.formal: He has submitted his resume.

words.simpleMeaning: relating to relationships and communication between people

sentences.daily: I have strong interpersonal bonds.
sentences.office: She has excellent interpersonal skills.
sentences.interview: I have good interpersonal and communication skills.
sentences.business: We have built strong interpersonal relationships.
sentences.formal: He has interpersonal competencies.
sentences.informal: I'm good with people — interpersonal stuff.

words.simpleMeaning: continuing to do something despite difficulties

sentences.daily: I have persistence in everything I do.
sentences.office: She has shown great persistence.
sentences.interview: I have persistence as a key strength.
sentences.business: We have persistence in pursuing our goals.
sentences.formal: He has demonstrated remarkable persistence.

words.simpleMeaning: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles

sentences.daily: I have high integrity.
sentences.office: She has always shown integrity.
sentences.interview: I have integrity as my core value.
sentences.business: We have a culture of integrity.
sentences.formal: He has maintained his integrity.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to deal with people tactfully

sentences.daily: I have used diplomacy wisely.
sentences.office: She has handled conflicts with diplomacy.
sentences.interview: I have handled tough situations with diplomacy.
sentences.business: We have used diplomacy in negotiations.
sentences.formal: He has shown exceptional diplomacy.
sentences.informal: A bit of diplomacy goes a long way.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to understand and share the feelings of others

sentences.daily: I have empathy for others.
sentences.office: She has shown empathy to her team.
sentences.interview: I have empathy as a leadership quality.
sentences.business: We have built empathy-driven products.
sentences.formal: He has demonstrated empathy.
sentences.informal: Having empathy makes you a better person.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to adjust to new conditions

sentences.daily: I have high adaptability.
sentences.office: She has shown adaptability in tough situations.
sentences.interview: I have adaptability as a key strength.
sentences.business: We have demonstrated adaptability.
sentences.formal: He has shown remarkable adaptability.
sentences.informal: Adaptability is key in today's world.

words.simpleMeaning: to use something to maximum advantage

sentences.daily: I have leveraged my skills well.
sentences.office: She has leveraged her network.
sentences.interview: I have leveraged data to drive decisions.
sentences.business: We have leveraged new technology.
sentences.formal: He has leveraged institutional resources.

words.simpleMeaning: to have a strong effect on something

sentences.daily: I have created a positive impact.
sentences.office: She has impacted team performance.
sentences.interview: I have made a measurable impact.
sentences.business: We have impacted the market.
sentences.formal: He has impacted policy decisions.

words.usageNote: 'have an impact/make an impact' career word।

words.simpleMeaning: the result or consequence of an action

sentences.daily: I have a positive outcome from this.
sentences.office: She has tracked the outcome of every project.
sentences.interview: I have delivered strong outcomes.
sentences.business: We have achieved the desired outcome.
sentences.formal: He has reported the outcomes formally.
sentences.informal: The outcome was better than expected.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan to achieve a goal

sentences.daily: I have a strategy for my studies.
sentences.office: She has a clear strategy.
sentences.interview: I have developed marketing strategies.
sentences.business: We have a growth strategy.
sentences.formal: He has implemented the strategy.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to think about the future with imagination and wisdom

sentences.daily: I have a clear vision.
sentences.office: She has a vision for the team.
sentences.interview: I have a five-year professional vision.
sentences.business: We have a vision for the company.
sentences.formal: He has articulated the institutional vision.
sentences.informal: I've always had a clear vision.

words.simpleMeaning: an important task or goal

sentences.daily: I have a personal mission.
sentences.office: She has completed her mission.
sentences.interview: I have a mission to add value.
sentences.business: We have a clear company mission.
sentences.formal: He has fulfilled the mission.
sentences.informal: My mission is to be the best.

words.simpleMeaning: a planned set of activities with a specific goal

sentences.daily: I have a project to finish.
sentences.office: She has managed three projects.
sentences.interview: I have led large-scale projects.
sentences.business: We have a new development project.
sentences.formal: He has submitted the project report.
sentences.informal: Got a big project this week.

words.simpleMeaning: a person or company that buys services

sentences.daily: I have a new client.
sentences.office: She has handled 20 clients.
sentences.interview: I have managed international clients.
sentences.business: We have retained all our clients.
sentences.formal: He has served the client's best interests.

words.simpleMeaning: a person or company that sells goods or services

sentences.daily: I have contacted the vendor.
sentences.office: She has managed vendor relationships.
sentences.interview: I have negotiated with vendors.
sentences.business: We have three approved vendors.
sentences.formal: He has reviewed the vendor contract.

words.simpleMeaning: income generated from business activities

sentences.daily: I have tracked my revenue.
sentences.office: She has grown the team's revenue.
sentences.interview: I have contributed to revenue growth.
sentences.business: We have record revenue this year.
sentences.formal: He has reported increased revenue.

words.simpleMeaning: financial gain after costs

sentences.daily: I have earned a profit.
sentences.office: She has improved the department's profit.
sentences.interview: I have contributed to profit margins.
sentences.business: We have made significant profit.
sentences.formal: He has reported increased profit.

words.simpleMeaning: a bill sent to a client for goods or services

sentences.daily: I have sent the invoice.
sentences.office: She has processed the invoice.
sentences.interview: I have managed invoice cycles.
sentences.business: We have issued 50 invoices.
sentences.formal: He has approved the vendor invoice.
sentences.informal: Just sent the invoice, waiting for payment.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan put forward for consideration

sentences.daily: I have a proposal idea.
sentences.office: She has submitted a proposal.
sentences.interview: I have written winning proposals.
sentences.business: We have a business proposal ready.
sentences.formal: He has presented the formal proposal.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal legal agreement between parties

sentences.daily: I have signed the contract.
sentences.office: She has drafted the contract.
sentences.interview: I have reviewed client contracts.
sentences.business: We have signed a three-year contract.
sentences.formal: He has executed the contract.

words.simpleMeaning: following rules or standards

sentences.daily: I have maintained compliance.
sentences.office: She has ensured regulatory compliance.
sentences.interview: I have experience in compliance management.
sentences.business: We have full compliance.
sentences.formal: He has reported compliance issues.

words.simpleMeaning: a rule made by a government or authority

sentences.daily: I have followed all regulations.
sentences.office: She has ensured compliance with regulations.
sentences.interview: I have expertise in data protection regulations.
sentences.business: We have adhered to regulations.
sentences.formal: He has cited the applicable regulations.
sentences.informal: Make sure you follow the regulations.

words.simpleMeaning: official permission to do something

sentences.daily: I have authorization to proceed.
sentences.office: She has given authorization.
sentences.interview: I have managed authorization protocols.
sentences.business: We have received the authorization.
sentences.formal: He has obtained written authorization.
sentences.informal: Got authorization, we can go ahead.

words.simpleMeaning: a meeting arranged for a specific time

sentences.daily: I have an appointment at 3 PM.
sentences.office: She has a doctor's appointment.
sentences.interview: I have an appointment for the interview.
sentences.business: We have confirmed the appointment.
sentences.formal: He has scheduled an appointment.

words.simpleMeaning: a document confirming achievement

sentences.daily: I have a certificate in yoga.
sentences.office: She has all necessary certificates.
sentences.interview: I have a certificate in Python.
sentences.business: We have industry certificates.
sentences.formal: He has submitted the certificate.

words.simpleMeaning: being moved to a higher position at work

sentences.daily: I have received a promotion.
sentences.office: She has earned a promotion.
sentences.interview: I have achieved three promotions.
sentences.business: We have announced promotions.
sentences.formal: He has been granted a promotion.

words.simpleMeaning: an increase in salary

sentences.daily: I have received an increment.
sentences.office: She has discussed increment with HR.
sentences.interview: I have received increments for good performance.
sentences.business: We have revised increments.
sentences.formal: He has received an annual increment.
sentences.informal: Got a good increment this year.

words.simpleMeaning: to officially leave a job

sentences.daily: I have decided to resign.
sentences.office: She has tendered her resignation.
sentences.interview: I resigned to pursue higher studies.
sentences.business: He has resigned from the board.
sentences.formal: He has submitted his resignation letter.

words.simpleMeaning: to move to a new place for work

sentences.daily: I have relocated to a new city.
sentences.office: She has relocated for the job.
sentences.interview: I am willing to relocate.
sentences.business: We have relocated our headquarters.
sentences.formal: He has relocated internationally.

words.simpleMeaning: a temporary position to gain work experience

sentences.daily: I have completed an internship.
sentences.office: She has mentored interns.
sentences.interview: I have two internships in my resume.
sentences.business: We have an internship program.
sentences.formal: He has successfully completed his internship.

words.simpleMeaning: happening or working at the workplace itself

sentences.daily: I have an onsite visit tomorrow.
sentences.office: She has worked onsite for years.
sentences.interview: I can work onsite.
sentences.business: We have onsite support.
sentences.formal: He has completed onsite verification.

words.simpleMeaning: combining two different things, e.g. office and remote work

sentences.daily: I have a hybrid schedule.
sentences.office: She has a hybrid work model.
sentences.interview: I am comfortable with hybrid work.
sentences.business: We have adopted a hybrid approach.
sentences.formal: He has advocated for hybrid models.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal meeting to assess an employee's work

sentences.daily: I have a performance review today.
sentences.office: She has conducted performance reviews.
sentences.interview: I have received positive performance reviews.
sentences.business: We have quarterly performance reviews.
sentences.formal: He has undergone a performance review.

words.simpleMeaning: Key Performance Indicator — a measurable goal

sentences.daily: I have set my KPIs.
sentences.office: She has tracked KPIs weekly.
sentences.interview: I have defined and met KPIs.
sentences.business: We have clear KPIs.
sentences.formal: He has reported on KPIs.
sentences.informal: KPIs are all green this month.

words.simpleMeaning: Return on Investment — profit from an investment

sentences.daily: I have calculated the ROI.
sentences.office: She has reported positive ROI.
sentences.interview: I have improved ROI by 25%.
sentences.business: We have strong ROI.
sentences.formal: He has submitted the ROI report.

words.simpleMeaning: able to grow or be expanded easily

sentences.daily: I have a scalable plan.
sentences.office: She has built a scalable process.
sentences.interview: I have designed scalable systems.
sentences.business: We have a scalable business model.
sentences.formal: He has proposed a scalable solution.

words.simpleMeaning: able to be maintained long-term without harming the environment

sentences.daily: I have sustainable habits.
sentences.office: She has sustainable work practices.
sentences.interview: I have contributed to sustainable growth.
sentences.business: We have a sustainable model.
sentences.formal: He has advocated for sustainable practices.

words.simpleMeaning: the people working in a company or industry

sentences.daily: I have joined the workforce.
sentences.office: She has trained the workforce.
sentences.interview: I have contributed to workforce development.
sentences.business: We have a skilled workforce.
sentences.formal: He has managed the entire workforce.

words.simpleMeaning: the gradual reduction in staff through resignation or retirement

sentences.daily: I am aware of attrition.
sentences.office: She has reduced attrition significantly.
sentences.interview: I have worked to reduce attrition.
sentences.business: We have low attrition.
sentences.formal: He has addressed attrition in his report.

words.simpleMeaning: a series of steps for completing a process

sentences.daily: I have a good pipeline of ideas.
sentences.office: She has managed the sales pipeline.
sentences.interview: I have experience with data pipelines.
sentences.business: We have a strong sales pipeline.
sentences.formal: He has presented the pipeline update.

words.simpleMeaning: being involved or committed to something

sentences.daily: I have high engagement in class.
sentences.office: She has improved team engagement.
sentences.interview: I have driven high employee engagement.
sentences.business: We have strong customer engagement.
sentences.formal: He has reported engagement metrics.

words.simpleMeaning: something that must be completed as part of a project

sentences.daily: I have a deliverable due today.
sentences.office: She has completed all deliverables.
sentences.interview: I have delivered all project deliverables on time.
sentences.business: We have defined clear deliverables.
sentences.formal: He has submitted the deliverables.
